channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A film festival bridges two communities at the U.S.-Mexico border
item.description: Audiences on both sides of the U.S.-Mexico border wall in Nogales, Arizona and Nogales, Sonora gathered to watch the same films at the same time at a cross-border film festival.
item.content:encoded: Audiences on both sides of the U.S.-Mexico border wall in Nogales, Arizona and Nogales, Sonora gathered to watch the same films at the same time at a cross-border film festival.
item.dc:creator: Nina Kravinsky

item.title: Shein buys Everlane, which sold millennials the dream of ethical, affordable luxury
item.description: Everlane's finances have faltered in recent years. But will the merger alienate Everlane's existing shoppers — or sway droves of Shein fans to trade up?
item.content:encoded:
Everlane's finances have faltered in recent years. But will the merger alienate Everlane's existing shoppers — or sway droves of Shein fans to trade up?

(Image credit: Smith Collection/Gado/Getty Images
)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Colbert initially avoided discussing politics on 'Late Night.' Then he leaned in
item.description: Last night, Stephen Colbert said goodbye to The Late Show after 11 years. We listen back to his '16 interview, in which he talked about embracing his "sharp and satirical and highly opinionated" side.
item.content:encoded: Last night, Stephen Colbert said goodbye to The Late Show after 11 years. We listen back to his '16 interview, in which he talked about embracing his "sharp and satirical and highly opinionated" side.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Jack Antonoff says 'this version of modernity is trash' in new Bleachers album
item.description: Jack Antonoff explores marriage, grief and the current moment in Bleachers' latest album, everyone for ten minutes.
item.content:encoded:
Jack Antonoff explores marriage, grief and the current moment in Bleachers' latest album, everyone for ten minutes.

(Image credit: Alex Lockett)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: A stranger's compliment helped her feel confident after chemo
item.description: When Pat Gentile began to grow out her hair after chemotherapy, she was nervous to go to work for the first time without a wig. An unexpected encounter with a convenience store stranger changed that.
item.content:encoded: When Pat Gentile began to grow out her hair after chemotherapy, she was nervous to go to work for the first time without a wig. An unexpected encounter with a convenience store stranger changed that.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kwerel

item.title: 'Widow's Bay' is cursed -- but manages to be both funny and frightening
item.description: In Apple TV's new horror-comedy, Matthew Rhys plays a mayor who wants to turn his New England island into a popular tourist destination. There's just one problem: The island may be a source of evil.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

In Apple TV's new horror-comedy, Matthew Rhys plays a mayor who wants to turn his New England island into a popular tourist destination. There's just one problem: The island may be a source of evil.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' ends with a swan song and a giant wormhole
item.description: With a dash of sci-fi, Colbert ended his CBS late-night show with a powerful musical sendoff featuring Paul McCartney, Elvis Costello and Jon Batiste.
item.content:encoded:
Late Show finale.'/>

With a dash of sci-fi, Colbert ended his CBS late-night show with a powerful musical sendoff featuring Paul McCartney, Elvis Costello and Jon Batiste.

(Image credit: Scott Kowalchyk)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Stephen Colbert exits 'The Late Show' with a mix of defiance and celebration
item.description: Colbert has approached the last weeks of his show on his own terms, with A-list guests like Tom Hanks, David Letterman and David Byrne — but the guests for tonight's finale remain a mystery.
item.content:encoded: Colbert has approached the last weeks of his show on his own terms, with A-list guests like Tom Hanks, David Letterman and David Byrne — but the guests for tonight's finale remain a mystery.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: 'It's one of my dreams,' Rose Byrne says of her comic turn on Broadway
item.description: Byrne is one of the few actors to receive both an Oscar and a Tony nomination in the same year —for the indie film If I Had Legs I'd Kick You, and for Fallen Angels on Broadway.
item.content:encoded:
.'/>

Byrne is one of the few actors to receive both an Oscar and a Tony nomination in the same year —for the indie film If I Had Legs I'd Kick You, and for Fallen Angels on Broadway.

(Image credit: Andreas Rentz)
item.dc:creator: Ann Marie Baldonado

item.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
item.description: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

(Image credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez)
item.dc:creator: Diego Ibarra Sánchez
